_meta.author: Claude (scheduled scorecard-refine-national)
_meta.note: FL federal House batch-3: Laurel Lee (R-15), Vern Buchanan (R-16, retiring), Greg Steube (R-17), Scott Franklin (R-18), Dale Holness (D-20 primary candidate). Evidence from House Clerk roll-call XML, congress.gov/GovTrack cosponsor lists, Heritage Action, NRA-PVF, SBA Pro-Life, FEC, local press. Key catches: Buchanan's gun deviations (Yea HR 8 universal background checks 2019+2021, public red-flag/bump-stock advocacy, NRA A->B); Franklin's on-record openness to civil unions; Lee pro-IVF cosponsorships + AIPAC receipts; Holness candidacy VERIFIED ACTIVE for the Aug 18 2026 FL-20 primary (Cherfilus-McCormick resigned 4/21/2026, no special election called).

profile.background: US Representative FL-15 since Jan 2023. Former Florida Secretary of State and circuit judge. Vice Chair, Committee on House Administration; Chair, Elections Subcommittee. Seeking reelection 2026 (GOP primary Aug 18) with Trump endorsement.

laurel-lee.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] Establishment-lane law-and-order conservative; signature strength is election integrity (SAVE Act cosponsor + Yea x2, sponsored H.J.Res.152 citizens-only-voting amendment and H.R.4494 Ensuring Faith in Our Elections Act, helmed Zuckerbucks hearing). Solid hard votes on Born-Alive, women's sports, HR 2 border/E-Verify, Laken Riley, CBDC ban, vaccine-mandate repeal; Nay twice on Ukraine aid. DEVIATIONS: restrictions-only on abortion (states-decide, rape/incest exceptions, declined federal 15-week ban; never a Life at Conception cosponsor); actively pro-IVF (cosponsored H.Res.1037 + Bipartisan IVF for Military Families Act); voted for FRA debt deal + FY24 omnibus (Heritage 118th 74%, lifetime 84%); lead sponsor of FISA 702 reauth (RISAA); ~\$35k AIPAC PAC receipts since 2022. Zero abortion-lobby money (FEC verified; RFFA scores her 0%). No public profession of faith found.

0.note: No personhood/life-at-conception statement found; not a Life at Conception Act cosponsor in 118th or 119th (full cosponsor list checked).

1.note: Restrictions-only: says abortion 'belongs with the states,' favors rape/incest/life exceptions, declined to endorse a federal 15-week ban. Did vote Yea on Born-Alive (Roll 29-2023, Roll 27-2025) and OBBBA PP-defund.

2.note: Actively pro-IVF: cosponsored H.Res.1037 (118th, pro-IVF resolution) and H.R.6797 Bipartisan IVF for Military Families Act (119th) with no embryo-protection caveat.

4.note: FEC itemized receipts (committee C00815373): zero from Planned Parenthood/EMILY's/NARAL. Reproductive Freedom for All scores her 0%.

0.note: No marriage-definition statement found; House bio and campaign site contain no marriage/faith language.

1.note: Took office Jan 2023, after the Respect for Marriage Act votes; no subsequent statement found.

2.note: Yea on Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act twice (HR 734 Roll 192-2023; HR 28 Roll 12-2025); cosponsor of Protection of Women in Olympic and Amateur Sports Act (HR 1028, 119th).

4.note: Cosponsor, Dismantle DEI Act (HR 8706/118, HR 925/119) and Eliminate DEI in Colleges Act; Yea on both women's-sports bills.

0.note: Cosponsor, Educational Choice for Children Act (HR 531/118th, HR 833/119th) plus two charter-school bills. No homeschool-specific evidence.

1.note: Aye on HR 5 Parents Bill of Rights (Roll 161, 3/24/2023). No cosponsorship of minors'-gender-intervention bills found.

2.note: Cosponsor of Dismantle DEI Act, Eliminate DEI in Colleges Act, and Stopping Teachers Unions from Damaging Education Needs Today Act (HR 4705/118th).

3.note: Not a SCREEN/CASE Act cosponsor; her online-safety bill (HR 6289) is an FTC education campaign, not age-verification.

4.note: No faith-based-adoption position found.

0.note: No public profession of faith found; House bio and campaign site contain zero faith references.

1.note: Aye on HR 497 Freedom for Health Care Workers Act (repeal CMS vaccine mandate, Roll 98-2023); cosponsor of HR 7 (conscience/Hyde protections).

2.note: Mixed signals: Yea on Antisemitism Awareness Act (a bill some speech conservatives opposed) and lead sponsor of FISA 702 reauth (RISAA); no Christian-conviction compelled-speech position found.

4.note: No evidence found.

0.note: Aye on HR 5403 CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (Roll 230, 5/23/2024) AND cosponsor.

1.note: Not a Federal Reserve Transparency Act cosponsor; no sound-money position found.

2.note: Aye on Fiscal Responsibility Act debt-ceiling deal (Roll 243-2023), Yea on Sept 2023 CR and the \$1.2T FY24 omnibus (Roll 102-2024) — all Heritage-scored against; Heritage 118th score 74%.

4.note: Yea on H.J.Res.30 disapproving Biden DOL ESG-investing rule (Roll 124, 2/28/2023).

0.note: No hand-count/anti-machine position; as former FL Secretary of State she defends Florida's (machine-tabulated) model.

1.note: Aye on SAVE Act (HR 8281 Roll 345-2024; HR 22 Roll 102-2025), cosponsor of both; sponsored H.J.Res.152 constitutional amendment restricting voting to citizens; Aye on HR 192 (no noncitizen voting in DC).

2.note: No single-day-voting position; supports Florida's early/mail model with safeguards ('increase election access AND transparency').

3.note: Backed ACE Act (prohibits ballot harvesting and auto-mailed ballots); her HR 4494 sets election-mail rules and noncitizen list-maintenance.

4.note: Supported ACE Act incorporating the End Zuckerbucks Act (strips tax exemption for private election-administration funding); helmed the Zuckerbucks hearing as Elections Subcommittee chair; HR 4494 bans federal agencies funding NGO voter-registration drives.

0.note: Yea on HR 2 Secure the Border Act (wall construction, Roll 209, 5/11/2023).

1.note: No mandatory-deportation position beyond the HR 2 framework found.

2.note: No sanctuary-preemption vote or statement found.

3.note: Yea on HR 2, which mandates E-Verify for all employment (Title III). Also Yea + cosponsor on Laken Riley (Roll 66-2024; Roll 23-2025).

4.note: Farmland half documented (cosponsor HR 558, barring adversary-nation purchases near sensitive sites); no birthright-citizenship position found — held to null on the compound question.

0.note: Cosponsor of HR 38 concealed-carry reciprocity (permit-based); no permitless constitutional-carry position found.

1.note: NRA-PVF 'AQ' grade + endorsement (2022, questionnaire-based alignment against gun-control measures); no contrary votes or statements found.

2.note: Not a cosponsor of NFA-repeal bills; no position found.

3.note: No castle-doctrine position found (state-law issue; no federal action).

4.note: Yea on H.J.Res.44 disapproving the ATF pistol-brace rule (Roll 252, 6/13/2023); Yea on Veterans 2nd Amendment Protection Act (Roll 190-2026); cosponsor of Firearms Interstate Commerce Reform Act.

0.note: No Article I war-powers position found.

1.note: Nay on H.Con.Res.21 Syria-withdrawal war-powers resolution (Roll 136-2023) and Nay on H.Con.Res.75 (Roll 170-2026) — against withdrawal.

2.note: Voted No twice on Ukraine aid (HR 8035 \$61B Roll 151-2024; HR 2913 Roll 207-2026, not among the 18 R crossovers). Voted Yes on Israel supplementals (Roll 577-2023, Roll 152-2024).

3.note: FEC itemized receipts: AIPAC PAC contributions ~\$35k+ since 2022 (committee C00815373; \$5k 12/2022, ~\$30k across 2024 cycle, continuing into 2025-26).

4.note: No WHO/UN/NATO position found.

0.note: Aye HR 497 (repeal healthcare-worker vaccine mandate), Yea Pandemic is Over Act (Roll 100-2023), Yea H.J.Res.7 ending the COVID national emergency (Roll 104-2023).

1.note: No PREP Act/NCVIA position found.

2.note: No Big Ag antitrust position found.

3.note: No raw-milk/small-farm position found (only generic Supporting Farm Operations Act cosponsorship).

4.note: Not an Audit the Pentagon Act cosponsor; no position found.

profile.background: US Representative since 2007 (FL-13, then FL-16). Vice Chair, Ways & Means; Chair, W&M Health Subcommittee. Businessman. Announced Jan 27, 2026 he will not seek reelection — FL-16 open in 2026.

vern-buchanan.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] RETIRING: announced 1/27/2026 he will not seek reelection; serving out his term (FL-16 open seat). Business-Republican moderate — Heritage Action lifetime 64% (as low as 41% in the 115th). Solid pro-life restriction votes (Nay WHPA x2, Yea Born-Alive 2025, Nay embryonic stem-cell 2007) but never a Life at Conception cosponsor in 3 straight Congresses. Nay on Respect for Marriage Act BOTH votes (July + Dec 2022), Nay Equality Act. KEY CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS (guns): Yea on HR 8 universal background checks in 2019 AND 2021 (one of only 8 Republicans in 2021); 2019 letter to McConnell urging passage of background checks and consideration of red-flag laws; early bump-stock-ban advocate; NRA grade dropped A to B by 2020 — yet held the line Nay on the 2022 AWB, federal ERPO, and Safer Communities Act. Also: Yea on the \$61B Ukraine supplemental; voted to certify the 2020 election (Nay on both Jan 6 objections); Balanced Budget Amendment is his first bill every Congress since 2007.

0.note: Self-stated: 'I am pro-life. I will work hard to defend life from conception to natural death.'

1.note: Restrictions record only (Nay WHPA Rolls 295-2021/360-2022; Yea Born-Alive HR 21 Roll 27-2025; Yea 20-week ban 2015) — never a Life at Conception Act cosponsor (117th/118th/119th checked); no abolition advocacy found.

2.note: Nay on Stem Cell Research Enhancement Act (embryonic stem-cell funding), Roll 443, 6/7/2007. No IVF position found.

3.note: No euthanasia vote or specific statement found beyond the generic 'natural death' campaign line.

4.note: No abortion-industry money surfaced, but FEC/OpenSecrets detail could not be verified this pass — left null.

0.note: No marriage-definition affirmation found.

1.note: Nay on Respect for Marriage Act both times (Roll 373 July 2022, Roll 513 Dec 2022 — verified in Clerk XML); Nay Equality Act (Roll 39-2021); No on ENDA (2007).

2.note: Yea on Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (Roll 192-2023) and Yea + cosponsor of HR 28 (Roll 12-2025).

4.note: Nay Equality Act (2021), Nay 2016 Maloney SOGI contractor amendment, No ENDA. Mild deviation: Yes on 2013 VAWA reauth containing LGBT provisions.

0.note: Cosponsor, Educational Choice for Children Act of 2025 (HR 833).

1.note: Aye on HR 5 Parents Bill of Rights (Roll 161, 3/24/2023).

2.note: No CRT/sex-ed-specific vote or statement found.

3.note: Not a SCREEN Act cosponsor; no age-verification position found.

4.note: No faith-based-adoption position found.

0.note: Baptist affiliation documented, but no public profession/advocacy statement found.

1.note: Aye on Freedom for Health Care Workers Act (repealing the CMS worker vaccine mandate — medical-conscience protection), Roll 98, 1/31/2023.

2.note: No compelled-speech position found.

4.note: No evidence found.

0.note: Aye on HR 5403 CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (Roll 230, 5/23/2024).

1.note: Cosponsored the Federal Reserve Transparency Act (Audit the Fed, HR 24) in the 114th Congress; NOT a cosponsor in the 118th/119th — older alignment, noted.

2.note: Balanced Budget Amendment is his first-filed bill every Congress since 2007 (H.J.Res.2 in the 119th). Heritage lifetime 64% reflects moderate spending votes elsewhere — noted.

4.note: Yea on H.J.Res.30 disapproving the DOL ESG retirement-investing rule (Roll 124, 2/28/2023).

0.note: No paper-ballot/anti-machine position found.

1.note: Aye on SAVE Act (Roll 345, 7/10/2024) and Yea on HR 22 (Roll 102, 4/10/2025) — proof of citizenship to vote.

2.note: No single-day-voting position found.

3.note: Nay on HR 1 'For the People Act' (federalized mass mail-in/automatic registration), Roll 62, 3/3/2021.

4.note: No Zuckerbucks position found. Context: voted to certify the 2020 election (Nay on both AZ/PA objections, Rolls 10-11, Jan 2021).

0.note: Yea on HR 2 Secure the Border Act (wall construction), Roll 209, 5/11/2023.

1.note: No mandatory-deportation position beyond HR 2 found.

2.note: No sanctuary-preemption position found.

3.note: Yea on HR 2 (mandatory E-Verify); Yea on Laken Riley Act (HR 29 Roll 6-2025 and final S.5 Roll 23-2025).

4.note: Not a Birthright Citizenship Act cosponsor; no farmland position found.

0.note: Original cosponsor of HR 38 concealed-carry reciprocity (permit-based, 2025); Aye on 2017 reciprocity (Roll 663). No permitless-carry position found.

1.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: Yea on HR 8 universal background checks 2019 (Roll 99) AND 2021 (Roll 75, one of 8 Rs); 2019 letter urging McConnell to pass background checks and consider red-flag laws like Florida's; early bump-stock-ban advocate; NRA grade fell A to B. Did vote Nay on the 2022 AWB (Roll 410), federal ERPO (Roll 255), and Safer Communities (Roll 299) — mixed record, scored on the documented red-flag/background-check advocacy.

2.note: No NFA/GCA-repeal position found.

4.note: No ATF-overreach vote found this pass; his bump-stock-ban advocacy is captured in q1.

0.note: No Article I war-powers position found.

1.note: Nay on HR 256 repealing the 2002 Iraq AUMF (Roll 172, 6/17/2021) — against AUMF retrenchment.

2.note: Yea on the \$61B Ukraine Security Supplemental (HR 8035, Roll 151, 4/20/2024). Also Yea Israel supplemental (Roll 152) and Indo-Pacific package.

3.note: Track AIPAC lists him as AIPAC-funded, but dollar amounts could not be verified against FEC/OpenSecrets this pass — held to null.

4.note: No WHO/UN/NATO position found.

0.note: Aye on repealing the healthcare-worker COVID vaccine mandate (HR 497 Roll 98-2023); Yea ending the COVID national emergency (H.J.Res.7 Roll 104) and Pandemic is Over Act (Roll 100).

1.note: No PREP Act/NCVIA position found. Context: chairs W&M Health Subcommittee; Health Professionals ~\$210k top industry donor 2023-24.

2.note: No Big Ag antitrust position found.

4.note: Not an Audit the Pentagon cosponsor; no position found.

profile.religion: Methodist; openly professes biblical faith (read Deuteronomy on the House floor during the Equality Act debate)
profile.background: US Representative FL-17 since 2019. Army JAG captain, Iraq veteran; former FL state senator. Judiciary and Veterans Affairs Committees. Running in the Aug 18, 2026 GOP primary.

greg-steube.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] Near-uniform Christian-conservative record — Heritage Action lifetime 95% (96% in the 119th), NRA A+ with endorsement. Life at Conception Act cosponsor in three straight Congresses with a personhood op-ed ('unborn children are entitled to the full protections of life under the law'); Nay twice on the Respect for Marriage Act; THE sponsor of the Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (HR 734/HR 28); SCREEN Act (porn age-verification) cosponsor x2; Audit-the-Fed cosponsor x2; Nay on the FRA debt deal; SAVE Act cosponsor; HR 2 + Laken Riley Yea; Birthright Citizenship Act cosponsor; original cosponsor of the Constitutional Hearing Protection Act (suppressors off the NFA); led legislation to reinstate troops discharged over the COVID vaccine mandate; GRACE Act sponsor (religious exemptions from school vaccine mandates). America-First: Nay on the \$60B Ukraine supplemental, Yea on Israel aid, cosponsor of No WHO Pandemic Treaty Without Senate Approval. CROSS-PARTY NOTE: Yea to repeal the 2002 Iraq AUMF (2021, one of ~49 Rs). Missed Feb 2023 votes (incl. anti-ESG CRA) recovering from a serious ladder fall — absence, not position.

0.note: Op-ed: 'unborn children are entitled to the full protections of life under the law'; stated position 'Human life begins at conception and deserves legal protection at every stage until natural death.'

1.note: Life at Conception Act cosponsor in the 117th (HR 1011), 118th (HR 431), and 119th (HR 722) — abolition-level advocacy. Also Yea on Born-Alive twice (Roll 29-2023, Roll 27-2025) and introduced HR 8284 barring funds to telehealth abortion-drug providers.

2.note: No embryonic-stem-cell/IVF position found (2024 IVF votes were Senate-only).

3.note: Only the generic 'until natural death' position line — no specific euthanasia vote or statement; held to null.

4.note: No abortion-industry money surfaced, but donor detail not verifiable this pass — left null.

0.note: No explicit marriage-as-man-woman affirmation located (his floor statements address sexual identity, not marriage definition).

1.note: Nay on the Respect for Marriage Act both times (July 19 and Dec 8, 2022 — verified in Clerk XML).

2.note: THE sponsor of the Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (HR 734/118th, HR 28/119th; Yea Roll 12-2025); floor statement affirming sexual identity 'biologically assigned by God.'

4.note: Public opposition to the Equality Act (floor speech, Feb 2021); NDAA amendments to stop DEI funding in the military.

0.note: Cosponsor, Educational Choice for Children Act in the 118th (HR 531) and 119th (HR 817).

1.note: Aye on HR 5 Parents Bill of Rights (Roll 161-2023); sponsor of the GRACE Act (religious exemptions from school vaccine mandates — parental/conscience consent).

2.note: Anti-DEI amendments target the military, not K-12 curricula; no K-12-specific CRT/sex-ed action found.

3.note: Cosponsor of the SCREEN Act (porn age-verification) in both the 118th (HR 6429) and 119th (HR 1623).

4.note: No faith-based-adoption position found.

0.note: Read Deuteronomy 22:5 on the House floor; 'The Equality Act is clearly not the direction of God' — open public profession of biblical faith in office.

1.note: Introduced the GRACE Act — Guaranteeing Religious Accommodation in Childhood Education (religious exemptions from school vaccine mandates).

2.note: No compelled-speech position found.

4.note: No evidence found.

0.note: Cosponsor AND Aye on the CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (HR 5403, Roll 230, 5/23/2024).

1.note: Cosponsor of the Federal Reserve Transparency Act (Audit the Fed, HR 24) in both the 118th and 119th.

2.note: Nay on the Fiscal Responsibility Act debt-limit deal (Roll 243-2023, the Heritage position); Heritage Action lifetime 95%.

4.note: Not Voting on the anti-ESG CRA (Roll 124-2023; recovering from his Jan 2023 ladder fall) — absence, not position.

0.note: No paper-ballot/anti-machine position found.

1.note: Cosponsor of the SAVE Act (HR 8281) and Aye on passage (Roll 345-2024); Yea again on HR 22 (Roll 102-2025).

2.note: No single-day-voting position found.

3.note: Nay on HR 1 'For the People Act' (Roll 62-2021); cosponsor of the ACE Act (HR 4563), which prohibits ballot harvesting and auto-mailed ballots.

4.note: Cosponsor of the ACE Act, which bans private election-administration funding (End Zuckerbucks provisions).

0.note: Yea on HR 2 Secure the Border Act (wall construction), Roll 209, 5/11/2023.

1.note: No mandatory-deportation position beyond HR 2 found.

2.note: No standalone sanctuary-preemption vote found beyond HR 2 provisions.

3.note: Yea on HR 2 (mandatory E-Verify, Title III); Yea on Laken Riley three times (Roll 66-2024, Roll 6-2025, final S.5 Roll 23-2025).

4.note: Cosponsor, Birthright Citizenship Act of 2025 (HR 569). Farmland half: no position found — scored on the lead clause.

0.note: Cosponsor of HR 38 reciprocity (permit-based); led gun-rights bills as a FL state senator — but no federal permitless-carry sponsorship found.

1.note: Nay on the federal red-flag act (Roll 255-2022), Nay on the Assault Weapons Ban (Roll 410-2022), Nay on the Safer Communities gun package (Roll 299-2022); NRA A+ with endorsement.

2.note: Original cosponsor of the Constitutional Hearing Protection Act (HR 3228, removes suppressors from the NFA) — direct NFA-rollback support.

3.note: No federal castle-doctrine action; state-level gun-rights leadership noted but not cell-specific.

4.note: Yea on H.J.Res.44 disapproving the ATF pistol-brace rule (Roll 252, 6/13/2023).

0.note: No Article I war-powers position found.

1.note: CROSS-PARTY: Yea on HR 256 repealing the 2002 Iraq AUMF (Roll 172, 6/17/2021), one of ~49 Republicans.

2.note: Nay on the \$60B Ukraine supplemental (Roll 151-2024, Heritage key vote); Yea on Israel aid (Roll 152-2024, Roll 577-2023).

3.note: AIPAC/foreign-lobby receipts not verifiable this pass — left null.

4.note: Cosponsor, No WHO Pandemic Preparedness Treaty Without Senate Approval Act (HR 1425, 118th). No UN/NATO position found.

0.note: Introduced legislation to reinstate (with backpay) troops discharged for refusing the COVID vaccine; GRACE Act religious exemptions from school vaccine mandates.

1.note: No PREP Act/NCVIA position found.

2.note: No Big Ag antitrust position found.

4.note: No Pentagon-audit position found.

profile.religion: Presbyterian — elder at First Presbyterian Church of Lakeland
profile.background: US Representative since 2021 (FL-15, then FL-18 from 2023). Retired Navy aviator (26 years, USNA '86). Appropriations Committee; Vice Chair, Ag-Rural Development-FDA Subcommittee. Running in the Aug 18, 2026 GOP primary.

scott-franklin.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] Reliable party-line conservative with a Navy-establishment streak. Nay on the Respect for Marriage Act both votes — but his own statement was federalism/conscience-framed and said he has no issue with government recognizing 'civil unions between any two persons' (biblical_marriage q1 FALSE: documented openness to civil unions). Life at Conception Act cosponsor in the 117th and 118th but dropped in the 119th; Yea Born-Alive, women's-sports x2, HR 2, Laken Riley x3, SAVE x2, CBDC ban, pistol-brace CRA; ACE Act + Audit-the-Fed + Birthright Citizenship Act cosponsor; NRA A/Endorsed (2024); elder at First Presbyterian Lakeland; voted to sustain the 2020 AZ/PA elector objections. DEVIATIONS (intra-coalition): Aye on the Fiscal Responsibility Act debt deal (Heritage 118th score 70%); Nay on 2024 Ukraine aid but anti-restraint on war powers (Nay AUMF repeal, Nay Syria withdrawal); AIPAC \$10k reported (unverified compilation). Silent on IVF, euthanasia, raw milk, Pentagon audit.

0.note: Cosponsored the Life at Conception Act (personhood from conception) in the 117th (HR 1011) and 118th (HR 431). NOT a cosponsor of the 119th version (HR 722) — post-Dobbs softening signal, noted.

1.note: Life at Conception Act cosponsorship (117th-118th) is abolition-level advocacy; also Yea + cosponsor on Born-Alive (HR 26, Roll 29-2023). Dropped LACA in the 119th — noted.

2.note: No embryonic-stem-cell/IVF position found.

4.note: No abortion-PAC money surfaced; donor detail not verifiable this pass — left null.

0.note: Never affirmed a man-woman definition; framed RFMA opposition as federalism ('the federal government getting into the business of defining marriage is an overreach').

1.note: Despite Nay on RFMA both times (Rolls 373/513-2022, conscience-framed), his own statement: 'I don't have issues with the government recognizing civil unions between any two persons for legal purposes' — documented openness to civil unions, contrary to opposing all such legal forms.

2.note: Yea on the Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act twice (HR 734 Roll 192-2023; HR 28 Roll 12-2025).

4.note: Not a Protect Children's Innocence Act cosponsor; no LGBTQ-promotion-specific vote or statement found beyond the sports votes (scored at q2).

0.note: Cosponsor, Educational Choice for Children Act (HR 531, 118th).

1.note: Aye on HR 5 Parents Bill of Rights (Roll 161, 3/24/2023).

2.note: No CRT/sex-ed-specific action found.

3.note: Not a SCREEN Act cosponsor; no age-verification position found.

4.note: No faith-based-adoption position found.

0.note: Public self-profession: 'A man of faith and family… active members of First Presbyterian Church of Lakeland, where Scott serves as an elder.'

1.note: RFMA statement explicitly defends 'the religious freedoms of those who might be compelled to provide services for gay marriages… against their convictions' — conscience-exemption advocacy.

2.note: No compelled-speech position found.

4.note: No evidence found.

0.note: Aye on HR 5403 CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (Roll 230, 5/23/2024).

1.note: Cosponsor of the Federal Reserve Transparency Act (Audit the Fed) in both the 118th and 119th.

2.note: Aye on the Fiscal Responsibility Act debt-ceiling deal (Roll 243-2023, Heritage key-voted against); Heritage 118th score 70%. Did vote Yea on Limit, Save, Grow (Roll 199-2023) — mixed, scored on the marquee deal vote.

4.note: Yea on H.J.Res.30 disapproving the DOL ESG-investing rule (Roll 124, 2/28/2023).

0.note: No paper-ballot/anti-machine position found.

1.note: Aye on the SAVE Act (Roll 345-2024) and Yea on HR 22 (Roll 102-2025) — citizenship proof to vote.

2.note: No single-day-voting position found.

3.note: Cosponsor of the ACE Act (HR 4563), which prohibits ballot harvesting and auto-mailed ballots.

4.note: ACE Act cosponsor — bans private election-administration funding (End Zuckerbucks provisions). Context: voted to sustain 2020 AZ/PA elector objections (Rolls 10-11, Jan 2021).

0.note: Yea on HR 2 Secure the Border Act (wall construction), Roll 209, 5/11/2023.

1.note: No mandatory-deportation position beyond HR 2 found.

2.note: No sanctuary-preemption position found.

3.note: Yea on HR 2 (mandatory E-Verify); Yea on Laken Riley three times (Roll 66-2024, Roll 6-2025, S.5 Roll 23-2025).

4.note: Cosponsor, Birthright Citizenship Act of 2025 (HR 569) and Equal Representation Act (citizens-only apportionment, HR 151). Farmland half: no position found — scored on the lead clause.

0.note: Cosponsor of HR 38 reciprocity (permit-based, 118th & 119th); no permitless-carry position found.

1.note: Nay on the Assault Weapons Ban (Roll 410-2022), Nay on the federal red-flag act (Roll 255-2022), Nay on the HR 7910 gun package (Roll 245-2022); NRA-PVF A grade + endorsement (2024).

2.note: No NFA/GCA-repeal position found.

4.note: Yea on H.J.Res.44 disapproving the ATF pistol-brace rule (Roll 252, 6/13/2023).

0.note: No Article I war-powers position found.

Signed the Sept 2022 letter demanding Pentagon answers on the military mandate.

set.office: U.S. Representative FL-20 (2026 D primary candidate · open seat · Cherfilus-McCormick resigned 4/21/2026)

profile.background: Former Broward County Commissioner (2010-2022) and Broward County Mayor (2019-20); former Lauderhill commissioner (2004-10). Jamaican-born realtor. Lost the 2021 FL-20 special D primary to Cherfilus-McCormick by 5 votes; now the FEC-fundraising leader in the 2026 FL-20 D primary (Aug 18, 2026).

dale-holness.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] CANDIDACY VERIFIED ACTIVE (2026-06-10): FEC Statement of Candidacy filed 8/2/2025 (committee C00776831, ~\$306k raised, \$313k cash on hand — field leader); actively campaigning per May 2026 press; running in the regular Aug 18, 2026 D primary. Seat vacant since Cherfilus-McCormick resigned 4/21/2026 (House Ethics disaster-relief-funds finding); as of latest reporting DeSantis had NOT called a special election. Establishment-equity Democrat with an 18-year local record: as Broward Mayor signed the July 2020 countywide mask mandate, curfew, and gathering-cap emergency orders (citations issued); at a 2021 CD-20 forum backed mandatory COVID vaccines and Biden's employer mandate, and endorsed packing the Supreme Court; runs on 'Prosperity for All' race-conscious economics (30-for-30 set-asides) and Haitian TPS advocacy. Campaign Issues page silent on abortion/LGBTQ/guns; only abortion data point is 2021 forum agreement on 'abortion rights' (no PP endorsement found). CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS: vocally pro-sheriff (endorsed Sheriff Gregory Tony, praised \$30M training investment; never embraced defund); security-acknowledging immigration framing ('rules that protect our country'); visible faith-community ties (pastor-administered swearing-in). Integrity context: daughter sentenced to 20 months for \$300k PPP fraud (2021-22); no charges against Dale.
